examples: The land around here is flat.

definitions.text: In a horizontal line or plane; not sloping.
Smooth; having no protrusions, indentations or other surface irregularities, or relatively so.
examples: The surface of the mirror must be completely flat.
The carpet isn't properly flat in that corner.
Spread the tablecloth flat over the table.
I asked him if he wanted to marry me and he turned me down flat.
In the mile race, Smith's time was 3:58.56, and Brown's was four minutes flat.

definitions.text: An area of level ground (sometimes covered with shallow or tidal water).
examples: The hovercraft skimmed across the open flats.
the eastern end of the salt flat; mud flat, tidal flat, flood flat
I can run on the flat but not up hills.
The going will be easier once we're through these mountains and onto the flat.

definitions.text: Level horse-racing ground, as contrasted with courses incorporating jumps, or the racing done on such ground.
examples: This horse will do better over the flat.

definitions.text: To make a flat call; to call without raising.
To become flat or flattened; to sink or fall to an even surface.
A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent
